search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG150 migraine follow-up prophylaxis review recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
Commercial tools are not authoritative clinical workflow sources.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for people aged 12 years and over with migraine; the legacy workflow has no explicit age restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for primary and specialist migraine review, with emergency assessment and treatment decisions remaining clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review, local prescribing governance, and local referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current official CG150 recommendations, last updated 2025-06-03, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG150 remains the current official NICE headache and migraine guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped focal-neurological, pattern-change, vomiting, and clinician-led imaging fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped migraine frequency, duration, severity, aura, triggers, impact, medication-use, treatment-response, menstrual-relation, associated-symptom, and medication-overuse fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped headache-diary, structured-history, referral, and arranged-review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped documentation-only acute and preventive plan fields without medicine names or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation that an acute or preventive migraine plan was clinician-decided and that prophylaxis was reviewed; no medicine name or dose is mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: People younger than 12 years are outside the exact source.

Detailed neurological, cranial-nerve, blood-pressure, and fundoscopy fields, blood tests, lifestyle, bridge therapy, supplements, generic clinician plans, telemedicine, and fixed follow-up intervals remain unsupported.
Medicine choice, dose, contraindications, imaging interpretation, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
